Let me compare the before and after states more carefully.
Before SPACE:
- id=4: B (black), 40 pixels, L-shape at [15-23, 18-26]
- id=5: c (charcoal), 45 pixels, L-shape at [15-23, 36-44]
- id=11: Y (yellow), 45 pixels, L-shape at [45-53, 51-59]
After SPACE:
- id=5: B (black), 40 pixels, L-shape at [15-23, 18-26]
- id=6: c (charcoal), 45 pixels, L-shape at [15-23, 36-44]
- id=12: Y (yellow), 45 pixels, L-shape at [45-53, 51-]
So the L-shapes stayed in the same positions! But the colors changed:
- The black L-shape (id=4) became id=5, still black, same boundary
- The charcoal L-shape (id=5) became id=6, still charcoal, same boundary
- The yellow L-shape (id=11) became id=12, still yellow, same boundary
Wait, the boundaries are the same! So the L-shapes didn't change positions didn't change. What changed?
Before: id=4 was B, 40 pixels, boundary=[[15, 18], [15, 26], [23, 26], [23, 24], [18, 24], [17, 23], [17, 18]]
After: id=5 is B, 40 pixels, boundary=[[15, 18], [15, 26], [23, 26], [23, 24], [18, 24], [17, 23], [17, 18]]
So the black L-shape stayed at the same position. The colors stayed the same. What actually changed?
Let me look at the white pixels:
Before: id=6,7,8,9,10 were all W, 1 pixel each - inside the black L-shape
After: id=7,8,9,10,11 are W, 1 pixel each - still 1 pixel each
Before: id=6: W, 1 pixel, boundary=[[16, 19]]
After: id=7: W, 1 pixel, boundary=[[16, 19]]
Before: id=7: W, 1 pixel, boundary=[[16, 22]]
After: id=8: W, 1 pixel, boundary=[[16, 22]]
Before: id=8: W, 1 pixel, boundary=[[16, 25]]
After: id=9: W, 1 pixel, boundary=[[16, 25]]
Before: id=9: W, 1 pixel, boundary=[[19, 25]]
After: id=10: W, 1 pixel, boundary=[[19, 25]]
Before: id=10: W, 1 pixel, boundary=[[22, 25]]
After: id=11: W, 1 pixel, boundary=[[22, 25]]
So the white pixels stayed in the same positions too!
What about the yellow bar on the right?
Before: id=3: Y, 64 pixels, boundary=[[0, 63]]
After: id=3: B, 1 pixel, boundary=[[0, 63]]
Before: id=4: Y, 63 pixels, boundary=[[1, 63], [63, 63]]
After: id=4: Y, 63 pixels, boundary=[[1, 63], [63, 63]]
Hmm wait, the yellow bar (id=3) before was Y, 64 pixels, boundary=[[0, 63]] - that's just one pixel at [0, 63]. After it's B, 1 pixel, boundary=[[0, 63]] - so it changed from yellow to black.
And the bottom bar:
Before: id=12: B, 63 pixels, boundary=[[63, 0], [63, 62]]
After: id=13: B, 63 pixels, boundary=[[63, 0], [63, 62]]
So the bottom bar stayed black.
The only real change seems to be:
- The top-right corner pixel (0, 63) changed from yellow to black.
That's a very subtle change. Let me look more carefully at what changed.
Actually wait, let me re-examine. The id=3 before was Y, 64 pixels. That's the yellow bar on the right edge. After, id=3 is B, 1 pixel. And id=4 is Y, 63 pixels.
So it seems like the yellow bar (64 pixels total) split into:
-  - One black pixel at [0, 63] (the top corner)
  - 63 yellow pixels below it
This is a subtle change. Let me think the game might be about filling or matching L-shapes. Let me try pressing SPACE again to see if there's a pattern.
